description:
You are an incident responder who has just received a compressed evidence bundle from the SOC team.  
Your assignment is to:

1. Extract the gzip-compressed tar archive located at  
   /home/user/evidence/evidence_bundle.tar.gz  
   into the directory /home/user/forensics (create this directory if it is missing).

2. Immediately after the extraction, generate the file  
   /home/user/forensics/extraction.log  

   The log must follow **exactly** this specification:
   • One line per file that was extracted (directories must be ignored).  
   • Each line contains the **relative** path of the file with respect to /home/user/forensics.  
   • Lines must appear in strict ASCII alphabetical order.  
   • No leading “./”, no extra whitespace, tabs, or blank lines.  
   • The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e., the final line is terminated with “\n”).  

Example of the required layout (the actual file names below are the real ones you will see after extraction):

auth.log.2023-08-11  
nginx/access.log  
nginx/error.log  
syslog.2023-08-11⏎   ← (final newline after the last entry)

Restriction: accomplish the extraction **and** creation of extraction.log with one single command invocation in the terminal (a command‐line pipeline is acceptable, but everything must be launched at once).

The automated checker will validate that:
• /home/user/forensics exists and is a directory.  
• The four evidence files are present in /home/user/forensics with exactly the same relative paths as inside the archive.  
• /home/user/forensics/extraction.log exists, is readable by the current user, and its contents match the specification above exactly (including the trailing newline and ordering).

No further changes are required. When you are satisfied that the extraction happened successfully and the log file meets the required format, you can consider the task complete.
truth:
Existing before the task starts:
Directory: /home/user/evidence
File: /home/user/evidence/evidence_bundle.tar.gz  
  (This archive contains the following file hierarchy, ownership user:user, mode 0644 for files, 0755 for directories)

  ├─ auth.log.2023-08-11  
  ├─ syslog.2023-08-11  
  └─ nginx/  
      ├─ access.log  
      └─ error.log  

Contents of each internal file:  
1. auth.log.2023-08-11  
   “Aug 11 02:15:47 sshd[2318]: Accepted password for analyst from 10.0.0.42 port 55218 ssh2\n”  

2. syslog.2023-08-11  
   “Aug 11 02:16:02 kernel: audit: type=1105 msg=audit(1691727362.123:98): audit_pid=1 ...\n”  

3. nginx/access.log  
   “10.0.0.17 - - [11/Aug/2023:02:17:03 +0000] \"GET /index.html HTTP/1.1\" 200 612 \"-\" \"curl/7.58.0\"\n”  

4. nginx/error.log  
   “2023/08/11 02:17:07 [error] 5342#0: *1 open() \"/var/www/html/favicon.ico\" failed (2: No such file or directory)...\n”

After correct completion by the agent, the following must be true:

• Directory /home/user/forensics exists.  
• Files and their paths relative to /home/user/forensics:
    auth.log.2023-08-11  
    syslog.2023-08-11  
    nginx/access.log  
    nginx/error.log  
  Each file’s content must match exactly the strings given above.

• /home/user/forensics/extraction.log exists with mode 0644 and content:  
    auth.log.2023-08-11\n  
    nginx/access.log\n  
    nginx/error.log\n  
    syslog.2023-08-11\n  

(The last entry is followed by a newline character.)

No other files should be created or modified.
